topic_title: The ReAct pattern — Reason, Act, Observe, Repeat

sequence_drift.detail: No concepts from 14.7 (decision matrix: when to use agent vs simpler flow) or 14.8 (when NOT to use agents) are taught at depth. Agent frameworks (LangChain, AutoGen, LlamaIndex, OpenAI Assistants) are named as ReAct users — in scope. Max-cycle-count safety limit is an intrinsic ReAct loop mechanism — in scope. All new terms are in concepts_introduced or prior-topic carry-over vocab.

topic_scope.detail: §5.4 contrast with generic planning loop (14.5) is explicitly in scope. Framework mentions are in scope. No decision matrix or agent-safety content found. No concept taught beyond the headline boundary.

findings.detail: 'multi-hop question answering' (§7.2) is used without a formal definition; an inline illustration ('questions that require gathering evidence from more than one source and combining it') is provided immediately after. Adequate for comprehension but a formal one-phrase gloss would be cleaner.

beginner_readability.note: chain-of-thought reasoning defined on first use with abbreviation (CoT) in §5.1. Thought/Action/Observation/Final Answer each introduced one at a time under separate headings. Prompting strategy defined in §5.3. All prior-topic vocab (parametric memory, hallucination, RAG, etc.) carried over correctly without re-definition.

verdict: PASS — all 6 checks passed. Word count 3380 (within standard 3000–4400 range). All 5 primary resources cited; citation density in §5/§6/§7 approximately 95%, well above 60% threshold. No forward references to 14.7+ concepts. No scope creep: decision matrix and agent safety are absent; §5.4 ReAct-vs-generic-loop contrast is in scope. One soft readability finding: 'multi-hop question answering' (§7.2) relies on inline illustration rather than a formal gloss — below the 3-finding escalation threshold. Corpus is ready to ground downstream artifacts.
